Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A skilled threat actor launches a targeted attack against water and wastewater systems using phishing to gain initial access, exploits vulnerabilities in public-facing applications, and manipulates operational data to cause physical damage or service disruption.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-26919 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested vulnerability related to phishing attacks
stage.description: The threat actor initiates spear-phishing campaigns targeting employees with malicious email attachments or links. These emails exploit social engineering techniques to trick recipients into divulging credentials or executing payloads, enabling initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-27924 [CWE-XXX]: Vulnerability in SCADA/HMI interfaces allowing command execution
stage.description: The attacker exploits unpatched vulnerabilities in publicly accessible SCADA or HMI systems (e.g., default credentials, outdated firmware) to execute arbitrary commands on the network, leveraging legacy systems with minimal security hardening.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical operational parameters (e.g., chemical dosing levels, pump speeds) in the SCADA system are altered to cause physical damage or service disruption, exploiting poor segmentation between IT/OT networks to propagate effects across the infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: A skilled threat actor compromises water/wastewater systems through phishing, exploits public-facing application vulnerabilities, and manipulates operational data to disrupt services, highlighting risks in legacy OT environments with inadequate security measures.
